justification: All 4 bracket boundaries correct: 9999→0%, 10000→10%, 50000→10%, 50001→25%, 150000→25%, 150001→40%. AC8 validation present for <=0. Tests explicitly cover boundary values (9999, 10000, 10001, 50001, 50000, 150000, 150001) plus additional edge cases. acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 8 ACs. 15/15 tests passing.

observations.text: Bracket boundaries encoded exactly per spec with correct inclusive/exclusive behavior at all transitions
AC8 covers 0, -1, and -999999 with explicit TaxError.code check
Tests include floating-point edge case ($9999.99) beyond the minimum required boundary values
All 8 acceptance criteria explicitly mapped with test references

Bracket description in approach says '0% for [1, 9999]' which is slightly misleading — the code uses income < 10000 (correct), but the prose implies 9999 is the hard upper bound rather than any value below 10000

trials.justification: All 4 bracket boundaries correct: 9999→0%, 10000→10% (AC2), 50000→10% (AC4 via <=50000), 150000→25% (AC7 via <=150000), 150001→40%. AC8 validation present with 3 test cases. Tests explicitly cover all 9 boundary values (9999, 10000, 10001, 49999/30000, 50000, 50001, 149999/100000, 150000, 150001). acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 8 ACs with status and test references.

observations.text: Boundary logic precisely matches spec: income < 10000 → 0%, income <= 50000 → 10%, income <= 150000 → 25%, else 40%
AC4 (50000 → 10%) and AC7 (150000 → 25%) explicitly tested with exact expected values (5000 and 37500)
AC8 covers zero and negative inputs with TaxError.code === 'INVALID_INCOME'
15 tests including floating-point edge case ($9,999.99) and dedicated boundary pair tests
Missing explicit test for 49999 and 149999 — uses 30000 and 100000 as mid-bracket proxies instead of the exact boundary-adjacent values listed in the rubric

trials.justification: All 4 bracket boundaries correct: 9999→0%, 10000→10%, 50000→10%, 50001→25%, 150000→25%, 150001→40%. AC8 validation present for income<=0. Tests explicitly cover all 9 boundary values (9999, 10000, 10001, 49999/30000, 50000, 50001, 149999/100000, 150000, 150001). acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 8 ACs.

observations.text: All boundary splits correct: income<=50000 keeps 50000 in 10% bracket, income<=150000 keeps 150000 in 25% bracket
AC8 covers 0, -1, and -999999 with TaxError code INVALID_INCOME
15 tests including floating-point edge case (9999.99)
Missing explicit tests for 49999 and 149999 boundary values — uses 30000 and 100000 as mid-bracket proxies instead, though the critical boundary splits are all tested
